text:
CT CHEST/ABDOMEN/PELVIS WITH IV CONTRAST

CLINICAL INDICATION: 64-year-old female with Stage IIIB left upper lobe lung adenocarcinoma, EGFR L858R-mutant. Prior treatment history includes definitive concurrent chemoradiation, osimertinib consolidation (discontinued at disease progression), SRS to brain metastases, carboplatin/pemetrexed x6 cycles, and brief pemetrexed maintenance. Currently enrolled on Phase I/II clinical trial with tepotinib and osimertinib combination (MET amplification/EGFR L858R-T790M resistance mechanism). On-trial restaging CT at approximately 8 weeks of combination therapy.

TECHNIQUE: Multidetector CT of the chest, abdomen, and pelvis acquired with oral and IV contrast in the portal venous phase. 1.25 mm axial reconstructions with multiplanar reformats. Dose modulation applied. Comparison made to prior CT from approximately 8 weeks ago.

---

FINDINGS:

CHEST:

Lungs and Airways: The left upper lobe demonstrates a persistent area of consolidation/soft tissue opacity in the anterior segment, now measuring approximately 1.2 x 0.9 cm in its greatest axial dimensions. This has visibly decreased compared with the prior study where this measured approximately 1.8 x 1.4 cm. The appearance continues to be somewhat heterogeneous, with irregular margins and adjacent linear densities consistent with the expected post-radiation fibrotic changes in this region -- the fibrotic component is difficult to fully separate from residual viable tumor. Overall, the dominant soft tissue component appears substantively smaller. The left upper lobe satellite nodule previously visible at the time of initial diagnosis has remained absent on this and recent prior studies.

A right lower lobe nodule was present on the study performed prior to initiation of chemotherapy, measuring 0.9 cm, and had subsequently decreased to 0.5 cm after carboplatin/pemetrexed. On the present examination, this nodule is not reliably identified; it may be below the threshold of confident detection or has resolved. No new pulmonary nodules are seen. No consolidation, pleural effusion, or pneumothorax identified. Airways are patent to the segmental level bilaterally.

Mediastinum and Hila: Mediastinal contour is unremarkable given prior radiation changes. Right paratracheal soft tissue (station 4R) continues to appear fibrotic/post-treatment and is stable at approximately 0.7 cm -- no worrisome change. Subcarinal region without significant adenopathy. Left hilar region with residual post-treatment fibrotic stranding, stable.

Pleura: No pleural effusion. No pneumothorax. Pleural surfaces are smooth bilaterally.

Heart and Pericardium: Heart size is within normal limits. No pericardial effusion.

Bony thorax: No suspicious lytic or sclerotic lesions in the imaged ribs, thoracic spine, sternum, or clavicles.

ABDOMEN AND PELVIS:

Liver: The liver is normal in size and attenuation. No focal hepatic lesions identified. No biliary ductal dilatation.

Spleen: Unremarkable, no focal lesions.

Pancreas: The pancreatic parenchyma is homogeneous without focal mass or ductal dilatation.

Adrenal Glands: The right adrenal gland is now normal in appearance. The previously noted right adrenal nodule measuring 0.7 cm (and 0.6 cm on the last CT) is no longer identified, consistent with resolution. The left adrenal gland is unremarkable.

Kidneys: Both kidneys enhance symmetrically without hydronephrosis or focal renal mass.

Gastrointestinal: The stomach, small bowel, and large bowel are within normal limits. No bowel obstruction. Appendix not separately identified but no periappendiceal inflammatory changes.

Lymph Nodes: No pathologically enlarged retroperitoneal, mesenteric, or pelvic lymph nodes.

Pelvis: Uterus and ovaries are atrophic, consistent with patient age and menopausal status. No free pelvic fluid. Bladder is adequately distended and unremarkable.

Vascular: The aorta is normal caliber without aneurysm. Portal and hepatic veins are patent.

Musculoskeletal: No aggressive osseous lesions in the imaged lumbar spine, sacrum, or pelvis. Mild multilevel lumbar degenerative changes.

---

IMPRESSION:

1. Continued decrease in the left upper lobe soft tissue opacity compared with the prior examination, now approximately 1.2 x 0.9 cm vs. prior 1.8 x 1.4 cm. This represents a meaningful interval reduction in the dominant residual thoracic lesion on the current clinical trial regimen. Post-radiation fibrotic changes in the surrounding parenchyma persist, as expected.

2. Right lower lobe nodule not confidently identified on this examination, representing either resolution or below-threshold detectability compared with the prior study where it measured 0.5 cm.

3. Resolution of the right adrenal nodule previously characterized as indeterminate/suspicious; this is no longer seen on the current examination.

4. No new intrathoracic, abdominal, or pelvic lesions to suggest new sites of metastatic involvement.

5. Stable post-radiation/fibrotic mediastinal and left upper lobe parenchymal changes.

Overall findings are favorable relative to the imaging obtained prior to trial enrollment, indicating ongoing response to the current combination targeted therapy.
